label: Interior painting walkthrough
intro: A bounded, contractor-style walkthrough for interior painting. This is the proof-phase script foundation and should be refined with real painter input before public launch.

nodes.title: Confirm the rooms in scope
nodes.prompt: Tell me which room or rooms you want painted first. If it is easier, just name the main room and we can expand from there.
nodes.helpText: Start with the smallest complete scope you want priced.

nodes.title: Capture the room overview
nodes.prompt: Point the camera at the room and slowly pan from left to right so I can see the walls, trim, windows, and ceiling.
nodes.helpText: A single steady video is better than several quick clips.

nodes.title: Describe the wall condition
nodes.prompt: Are the walls mostly clean, do they need minor patching, or are there obvious cracks, peeling, stains, or nail damage I should plan around?

nodes.title: Check trim and detail complexity
nodes.prompt: Hold the camera on the trim, doors, and windows for a few seconds so I can gauge the prep and cut-in complexity.

nodes.title: Measure the paintable perimeter
nodes.prompt: Use AR to measure the room perimeter. If the room is open to another space, measure only the walls you want included in the bid.
nodes.helpText: Linear wall footage is one of the most important pricing inputs for painting.

nodes.title: Clarify ceiling scope
nodes.prompt: Do you want the ceiling included too, or just the walls and trim?

nodes.title: Branch on repair detail
nodes.prompt: Decide whether repair follow-up is needed.

nodes.title: Capture repair detail
nodes.prompt: What is driving the repair work most: settlement cracks, peeling paint, water staining, damaged drywall, or something else?

nodes.title: Confirm the inspection summary
nodes.prompt: Here is the scope I captured. Confirm it before pricing so the estimate reflects the right prep level, wall footage, and ceiling scope.
